summary: 9 products surfaced from HN comments across 23 qualifying stories.

items.name: Needle
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Needle: We Distilled Gemini Tool Calling into a 26M Model
items.summary: 26M tool-calling model for consumer devices; commenters wanted a live demo.
items.why_included: It was the clearest AI-native launch thread with practical deployment chatter.
items.evidence: Commenters asked for a live Needle playground, suggested a small VPS demo, and discussed running it in Transformers.js.

items.name: Quack
items.summary: DuckDB client-server protocol for remote DuckDB and DuckLake use cases.
items.why_included: It surfaced a concrete data-infra project with real catalog and server deployment questions.
items.evidence: Commenters asked about using Quack as DuckLake's catalog database, while maintainers linked an in-progress DuckLake PR.

items.name: Stalwart
items.source_story_title: Dead.Letter (CVE-2026-45185) – How XBOW found an unauthenticated RCE on Exim
items.summary: Mail and collaboration server suggested as an Exim replacement.
items.why_included: It was the strongest mail-infra alternative mentioned in the Exim security thread.
items.evidence: One commenter said they were looking at Stalwart to replace Exim, and another called it a fine security-focused option.

items.name: oxlint
items.summary: Fast JavaScript and TypeScript linter pitched as a simpler ESLint replacement.
items.why_included: It was a concrete developer-tool recommendation hidden inside a plugin review thread.
items.evidence: A longtime Obsidian user suggested oxlint as a faster, simpler near drop-in replacement for ESLint.

items.name: Scalyr
items.source_story_title: Why senior developers fail to communicate their expertise
items.summary: Log-analysis platform recommended as the better fit for this use case.
items.why_included: It was a blunt, concrete tool recommendation amid a discussion about developer expertise.
items.evidence: A commenter said log analysis is a horrible UI in Obsidian and told people to use Scalyr instead.

items.name: Prusa
items.source_story_title: Bambu Lab is abusing the open source social contract
items.summary: 3D printer brand praised as plug-and-play and open compared with Bambu.
items.why_included: It was the clearest hardware alternative in the Bambu backlash thread.
items.evidence: Commenters said Prusa is pretty much plug-and-play, highly recommended, and a better open-platform choice.

items.name: SpaceEngine
items.source_story_title: Rendering the Sky, Sunsets, and Planets
items.summary: Universe simulator recommended as a reference for sky and atmosphere rendering.
items.why_included: It was the strongest outside product reference in the graphics thread.
items.evidence: A commenter called SpaceEngine highly recommended and linked it as inspiration for atmospheric rendering.

items.name: Victron Phoenix Charger
items.summary: AC-to-12V charger used in a battery-backed UPS replacement setup.
items.why_included: It stood out as a practical hardware fix instead of a theoretical UPS debate.
items.evidence: A commenter said they replaced multiple UPS units with Victron AC-to-12V chargers and reported the setup worked well.

items.name: Micsig MDP probes
items.summary: High-voltage differential probes recommended as a good starter probe.
items.why_included: It was one of the few concrete test-equipment recommendations in the UPS measurement thread.
items.evidence: A commenter said Micsig makes a good starter probe that would be enough for mains testing.
